sections.title: Starting off
Talking to Irena again

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Please bring my daughter back to me. She is most likely list in the Desert somewhere. I miss her so much... Wahhhhh! *Sob*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where Ana went?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: I did look for her myself and a little way south. I came across some footprints which may be hers. I'm worried that she may have been caught by some guards and taken to work in that terrible desert mine to the south.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anywhere around here that she would go?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: I think she may have just gone a little way into the desert. It's unlike her to go too far away. She is an adventurous girl, but she knows how I worry. She wouldn't go too far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you think I should start my search?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: I think I saw some of her footprints to the south, you could start there. Hopefully she'll just be over a few sand dunes, but you never know. She may have been taken to that awful desert mining camp or captured by

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: brigands!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Looking at the footprints
Searching the footprints
Inspecting a cactus with a scarf on it

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Boo hoo! Oh dear, my only daughter....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Oh dear... my daughter, Ana, has gone missing in the desert. I fear that she is lost, or perhaps... *sob* even worse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When did she go into the desert?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: *Sob* she went in there just a few days ago, *Sob* she said she would be back yesterday.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: *Sob* And she's not...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll find her for you!

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Oh thank you! You've made me a very happy mother, I just hope it's not too late!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas on where she may have gone?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: I did go looking for her myself and I came across some footprints just a little way south. I'm worried that they lead to the desert mining camp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Well I hope you find her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cheer up, it might never happen.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: It may already have happened you thoughtless oaf! My daughter, Ana, has gone missing in the desert. I fear that she is lost, or perhaps... *sobs* even worse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When did she go into the desert?

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: *Sob* she went in there just a few days ago, *Sob* she said she would be back yesterday.

sections.title: Getting into the camp
Talking to a Mercenary
Talking to a mercenary again

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Yeah, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: It's none of your business now get lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps five gold coins will make it my business?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Don't try to fool me, you don't have five gold coins! Before you try to bribe someone, make sure you have the money effendi!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Guards!! Guards!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Let's see how good you are with Desert Survival techniques.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You won't be needing that water anymore!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, it certainly will help... -- The guard takes the five gold coins. -- Now then, what did you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: It's just a mining camp. Prisoners are sent here from Al Kharid. They serve out their sentence by mining. Most prisoners will end their days here, surrounded by desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you could almost say that they got their ... 'Just Desserts'

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: You could say that... -- There is an awkward pause --

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: But it wouldn't be very funny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When they talk about 'the silence of the desert', this must be what they mean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I take a look around the place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Not really. The Captain won't let you in the compound. He's the only one who has the key to the gate. And if you talk to him, he'll probably just order us to kill you. Unless...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unless what?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Unless he has a use for you. He's been trying to track down someone called 'Al Zaba Bhasim'. You could offer to catch him and that might put you in his good books?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find this Al Zaba Bhasim?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, he could be anywhere. He's a nomadic desert dweller. However, they say that he's frequently to be found to the west in the hospitality of the tenti's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Tenti's, who are they?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, we're not really sure what their proper name is. But they live in tents so we call them the 'Tenti's'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is Al Zaba Bhasim very tough?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure, but by all accounts, he is a slippery fellow. The Captain has been trying to capture him for years. A bit of a waste of time if you ask me. Anyway, I have to get going, I do have work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, if you have to know, we're making sure that no prisoners get out. -- The guard gives you a disapproving look. -- And to make sure that unauthorised people don't get in.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: -- The guard looks around nervously. -- You'd better go soon before the Captain orders us to kill you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the Captain order you to kill a lot of people?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: -- The guard snorts. -- *Snort* Just about anyone who talks to him.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Unless he has a use for you, he'll probably just order us to kill you. And it's such a horrible job cleaning up the mess afterwards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not to mention the senseless waste of human life.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Huh? Them's your words, not mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It doesn't sound as if you respect your Captain much.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, to be honest. We think he's not exactly as brave as he makes out. But we have to follow his orders. If someone called him a coward, or managed to trick him into a one-on-one duel many of us bet that he'd be

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: beaten.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how could I trick him into a one-on-one duel?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Like all cowards, he likes to be made to feel important. If anyone insults him outright, he just gets us to do his dirty work. However, if he thinks he's better than you, if you compliment him, he may feel that he can defeat

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: you. And if he initiated a duel, all the men agreed that they wouldn't intervene. We think he'd be slaughtered in double quick time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a bet on that?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Sorry, we've already taken your bet, wouldn't want any cheating now. Anyway, I have to get back to work. See ya around...

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, if you think you stand a chance, sure. But remember, if he gives us an order, we have to obey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bet 5 gold that I win.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Huh! It looks like you're betting with money you don't have.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Great, I'll take that bet.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Ok, if you win, you'll get 6 gold back. Anyway, I have to get going, I do have work to do. -- The guard walks off. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bet 10 gold that I win.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Huh! It looks like you're betting with money you don't have.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Great, I'll take that bet.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Ok, if you win, you'll get 12 gold back. Anyway, I have to get going, I do have work to do. -- The guard walks off. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bet 15 gold that I win.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Huh! It looks like you're betting with money you don't have.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Great, I'll take that bet.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Ok, if you win, you'll get 19 gold back. Anyway, I have to get going, I do have work to do. -- The guard walks off. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll bet 20 gold that I win.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Huh! It looks like you're betting with money you don't have.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Great, I'll take that bet.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Ok, if you win, you'll get 30 gold back. Anyway, I have to get going, I do have work to do. -- The guard walks off. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Yeah, whatever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Get lost before I chop off your head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps five gold coins will sweeten your mood?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Don't try to fool me, you don't have five gold coins! Before you try to bribe someone, make sure you have the money effendi!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, it certainly will help!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Now then, what did you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: It's just a mining camp. Prisoners are sent here from Al Kharid. They serve out their sentence by mining. Most prisoners will end their days here, surrounded by desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, if you have to know, we're making sure that no prisoners get out. -- The guard gives you a disapproving look. -- And to make sure that unauthorised people don't get in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman called Ana, have you seen her?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Hmm, well, we get a lot of people in here. But not many women though... Saw one come in last week....

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: But I don't know if it's the woman you're looking for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: It's just a mining camp. Prisoners are sent here from Al Kharid. They serve out their sentence by mining. Most prisoners will end their days here, surrounded by desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, if you have to know, we're making sure that no prisoners get out. -- The guard gives you a disapproving look. -- And to make sure that unauthorised people don't get in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman called Ana, have you seen her?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: No, now get lost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps five gold coins will help you remember?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Don't try to fool me, you don't have five gold coins! Before you try to bribe someone, make sure you have the money effendi!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, it certainly will help!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Now then, what did you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman called Ana, have you seen her?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Hmm, well, we get a lot of people in here. But not many women though... Saw one come in last week....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: It's just a mining camp. Prisoners are sent here from Al Kharid. They serve out their sentence by mining. Most prisoners will end their days here, surrounded by desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, if you have to know, we're making sure that no prisoners get out. -- The guard gives you a disapproving look. -- And to make sure that unauthorised people don't get in.

sections.title: Watching the mercenary captain
Attempting to enter the gate without the key
Searching the gate
Trying to attack a Mercenary

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Guards!! Guards!

sections.title: Trying to attack the Mercenary Captain

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Destroy this low life desert rat guards!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Guards! Attack that Desert Jackal!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Kill that intruder guards!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Men! Kill this filthy vermin.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Kill this madman guards!

sections.title: Talking to the Mercenary Captain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! A real captain!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Be off effendi, you are not wanted around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's rude, I ought to teach you some manners.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Oh yes! How might you do that? You seem little more than a gutter dweller. How could you teach me manners?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With my right fist and a good deal of force.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Oh yes, ready your weapon then! I'm sure you won't mind if my men join in? Har, har, har! Guards kill this gutter dwelling slime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, sorry. I thought I was talking to someone else.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Well, Effendi, you do need to be careful of what you say to people. Or they may take it the wrong way. Thankfully, I'm very understanding. I'll just let my guards deal with you.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Guards, teach this desert weed some manners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd love to work for a tough guy like you!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Hmmm, oh yes, what can you do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can mine!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha! You come to a mining camp and offer us your mining skills! Thanks effendi, but we have all the miners we'll ever need.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Now be off with you, before we reduce you to a bloody mess on the sand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I do something for a strong Captain like you?

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: You could bring me the head of Al Zaba Bhasim.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: He is the leader of the notorius desert bandits, they plague us daily. You should find them west of here. You should have no problem in finishing them all of. Do this for me and maybe I will consider helping you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Consider it done.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Good... run along then. You stand around flapping your tongue chatting like an insane camel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry Sir, I don't think I can do that.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Hmm, well yes. I did consider you might not be right for the job. Be off with you then before I turn my men loose on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a funny captain who can't fight his own battles!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Very well, if you're challenging me, let's get on with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be moving along then.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Effendi, I think you'll find that is the wisest decision you have made today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You there!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: How dare you talk to me like that! Explain your business quickly... or my guards will slay you where you stand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm lost, can you help me?

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: We are not a charity effendi. Be off with you before I have your head removed from your body.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't scare me!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Well, perhaps I can try a little harder. Guards, kill this infidel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you guarding?

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Effendi...

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: For just one second, imagine that it's none of your business!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Also imagine having your limbs pulled from your body one at a time.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Now, what was the question again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have sand in your ears? I said. 'What are you guarding?'

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Why... you ignorant, rude and eternally damned infidel, Guards, kill this infidel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey ugly!

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: I will not tolerate such insults.. Guards, kill [him/her].

sections.title: Guards eviction dialogue

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Prepare to die effendi!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Take that you infidel!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: We're sick of having to kill every lunatic that comes along and insults the captain, it makes such a mess. Thankfully, he's a bit decrepit so he doesn't notice so please, buzz off and don't come here again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Prepare to die effendi!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Are you mad effendi? This is your last chance! Leave now and never come back or I'll introduce you to my friend.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: And we'll be pleased to clean the mess up after you've been dispatched.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay, that does it!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're in serious trouble now!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay men, we need to teach this person a lesson

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: about desert survival.

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim after accepting to kill Al Zaba Bhasim

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: I am Al Shabim, greetings on behalf of the Bedabin nomads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for Al Zaba Bhasim.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Huh! You have been talking to the guards at the mining camp. Or worse, that cowardly mercenary captain. Al Zaba Bhasim does not exist, he is a figment of their imagination!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Go back and tell this captain that if he wants to find this man he should search for him personally. See how much of his own time he would like to waste.

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim again after being told Al Zaba Bhasim doesn't exist

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: I am Al Shabim, greetings on behalf of the Bedabin nomads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for Al Zaba Bhasim.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: I've already explained that he doesn't exist. Now, can we move on?

sections.title: Returning to the mercenary captain after learning about Al Zaba Bhasim

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've some information for you about Al Zaba Bhasim

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Oh yes and what might that be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Al Zaba Bhasim is a figment of your diseased imagination. Go look for him yourself and waste your own time if you think he still exists.

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Why, I've never been so insulted in all my days. Prepare to defend yourself wretch, I'll run you through myself!

sections.title: Attacking a mercenary
Killing the mercenary captain
Talking to the Mercenary to collect a bet
Talking to a guard again

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Move along now, we've had enough of your sort!

sections.title: Talking to a captain again

lines.speaker: Mercenary Captain
lines.text: Move along now... we've had enough of your sort!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I've come to collect my bet!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Well, I guess congratulations are in order.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: And we'll only charge the paltry sum of... erm...

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: The guards starts to do some mental calculations. You can see his brow furrow and he starts to sweat profusely.

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: Five gold for cleaning up the mess. You have won 1 Gold piece! Well done..! Ha, ha, ha ha! -- The guards walk off chuckling to themselves. --

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: 10 gold for cleaning up the mess. You have won 2 Gold pieces! Well done..! Ha, ha, ha ha! -- The guards walk off chuckling to themselves. --

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: 15 gold for cleaning up the mess. You have won 4 Gold pieces! Well done..! Ha, ha, ha ha! -- The guards walk off chuckling to themselves. --

lines.speaker: Mercenary
lines.text: 20 gold for cleaning up the mess. You have won 10 Gold pieces! Well done..! Ha, ha, ha, ha! -- The guards walk off chuckling to themselves. --

sections.title: Inside the camp
Entering the camp
Trying to enter the camp with a weapon equipped

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey - you with the weapon!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're not allowed in here!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Quick, men, get [him/her]!!

sections.title: Getting caught by guards when not wearing slave outfit

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, they're interesting clothes!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're no slave.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What are you doing in here?

sections.title: Getting jailed dialogue

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Into the cell you go! I hope this teaches you a lesson.

sections.title: Trying to open the prison door
Bend cell wall window
Escape cell wall window
Failing to climb rock after escaping the cell
Talking to the Mine cart driver

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Don't trouble me, can't you see I'm busy?

sections.title: Opening the mine door entrance

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, they're interesting clothes!

sections.title: Talking to Male slave by the mine door entrance

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: You look like a new 'recruit'. How long have you been here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've just arrived.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Yeah, it looks like it as well. It's a shame that I won't be around long enough to get to know you. I'm making a break for it today. I have a plan to get out of here! It's amazing in its sophistication.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are those big wooden doors in the corner of the compound?

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: They lead to an underground mine, but you really don't want to go down there.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I've only seen slaves and guards go down there, I never see the slaves come back up. At least up here you have a nice view and a bit of sun. -- The slave smiles at you and goes back to his work. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, that sounds interesting.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Yes, it is actually. I have all the details figured out except for one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that then?

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: -- The slave rattles the chains on his arms loudly. -- These bracelets, I can't seem to get them off. If I could get them off, I'd be able to climb my way out of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can try to undo them for you.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Really, that would be great... -- The slave looks at you strangely. -- Hang on a minute... I suppose you want something for doing this?

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: The last time I did a trade in this place, I nearly lost the shirt from my back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's funny you should say that actually.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: -- The slave looks at you blankly. --

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Yeah, go on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I can get the chains off, you have to give me something, okay?

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Sure, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want your clothes!

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Blimey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can dress like a slave and gain access to the mine area to scout it out.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: You're either incredibly brave or incredibly stupid. But what would I wear if you take my clothes? Get me some nice desert clothes and I'll think about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds awful.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Yeah, bunch of no hopers, tried to rob me blind. But I guess that's what you get when you deal with convicts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's ridiculous, you're talking rubbish.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: No, it's true, I can make a break for it If I can just get these bracelets off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Thanks... same to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that's a shame...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still, 'worse things happen at sea right?'

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: You've obviously never worked as a slave

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: ...in a mining camp...

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: ...in the middle of the desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose I'd better be getting on my way now...

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: -- The slave nods in agreement and goes back to work. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I've been here ages.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: That's funny, I haven't seen you around here before. You're clothes look too clean for you to have been here ages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, you caught me out.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Ah ha! I knew it! A new recruit then? It's a shame that I won't be around long enough to get to know you. I'm making a break for it today.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I have a plan to get out of here! It's amazing in its sophistication.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The guards allow me to clean my clothes.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Oh, a special relationship with the guards heh? How very nice of them. Maybe you could persuade them to let me out of here?

sections.title: Talking to the Male slave after he mentions the handcuffs

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Hello again, do you want to try and unlock my chains? I'd really appreciate it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, okay, let's give it a go.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Are you givin' in already?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just want to try something else.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Okay, if you want to try again, let me know.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Great! You did it! Do you want to trade clothes now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll trade.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert robe, a desert shirt and desert boots if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert robe and a desert shirt if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert robe and desert boots if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert shirt and desert boots if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert robe if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need desert boots if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: I need a desert shirt if you want these clothes off me.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Great! You have the Desert Clothes! -- The slave starts undressing right in front of you -- Okay, here's the clothes, I won't need them anymore.

lines.speaker: Escaping slave
lines.text: Right, I'm off! Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, good luck to you too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks...

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Okay, fair enough, let me know if you change your mind though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do some other things first.

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Okay, fair enough. Let me know when you want to give it another go.

sections.title: Talking to the Male slave after offering to undo the locks

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Do you still want to try and undo the locks for me?

sections.title: Talking to the male slave while wearing slave clothes

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Not much to do here but mine all day long.

sections.title: Talking to the male slave after he offers to trade

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Do you have the Desert Clothes yet?

sections.title: Talking to the male slave after losing the slave clothes

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Oh bother, I was caught by the guards again... Listen, if you can get me some Desert Clothes, I'll trade you for my slaves clothes again.. Do you want to trade?

sections.title: Talking to the escaping slave

lines.speaker: Escaping slave
lines.text: Not much to do here but mine all day long.

sections.title: Getting caught by a guard while talking to the slave

lines.speaker: Male slave
lines.text: Oh oh!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oi, what are you two doing?

sections.title: Searching the gates while inside the mining camp
Watch mine door entrance
Open mine door entrance
Talking to a rowdy slave

lines.speaker: Rowdy slave
lines.text: Hey, you're in for a good beating!

lines.speaker: Rowdy slave
lines.text: I'm gonna teach you some respect!

lines.speaker: Rowdy slave
lines.text: Oi! Are you looking at me!

lines.speaker: Rowdy slave
lines.text: Oi! I'm gonna give you some trouble!

lines.speaker: Rowdy slave
lines.text: You wanna piece of me?

sections.title: Getting a Tenti pineapple
Talking to the guard by the cave opening

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yeah, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er nothing really.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay... so move along and get on with your work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to mine in a different area.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, you want to work in another area of the mine eh? --The guard seems pleased with his rhetorical question.-- Well, I can understand that! A change is as good as a rest they say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh, fat chance of a rest for me.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You miserable whelp! Get back to work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sir, you're quite right sir.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Of course I'm right. And what goes around comes around as they say. And it's been absolutely ages since I've had anything different to eat.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What I wouldn't give for some whole, fresh, ripe and juicy pineapple for a change. And those Tenti's have the best pineapple in this entire area.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm sure you get my meaning...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I going to get some pineapples around here?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well, that's not my problem is it? Also, I know that you slaves trade your items down here. I'm sure that if you're resourceful enough, you'll come up with the goods.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Now, get along and do some work, before we're both in for it. And remember, I prefer my pineapples whole, not chopped up with all the juice gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sir, we understand each other perfectly.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay, good then. And remember, I prefer my pineapples whole, not chopped up with all the juice gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the Tenti's?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well! You don't come from around here do you? The Tenti's are the nomadic people west of here. They live in tents, so we call them the Tenti's. They have great pineapples! I'm sure you get my meaning?

sections.title: The quest for Tenti's
Talking to Bedabin nomad

lines.speaker: Bedabin Nomad
lines.text: Hello Effendi! How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I could get a tenti pineapple?

lines.speaker: Bedabin Nomad
lines.text: Oooh, sir is looking for a really very special item then! You must go and talk to our very illustrious leader, Al Shabim. He's located in the largest tent in our village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: I am Al Shabim, greetings on behalf of the Bedabin nomads. Now... what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for a pineapple.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Oh yes, well that is interesting. Our sweet pineapples are renowned throughout the whole of Kharid ! And I'll give you one if you do me a favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes?

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Captain Siad at the mining camp is holding some secret information. It is very important to us and we would like you to get it for us. It gives details of an interesting, yet ancient weapon.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: We would gladly share this information with you. All you have to do is gain access to his private room upstairs. We have a key for the chest that contains this information. Are you interested in our deal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm interested.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: That's great Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Here is a copy of the key that should give you access to the chest.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Bring us back the plans inside the chest, they should be sealed. All haste to you Effendi!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at the moment.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Very well Effendi!

sections.title: After losing the Bedabin key

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: How are things going Effendi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the key!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: How very careless of you! Here is another key, don't lose it this time !

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Very well, good day Effendi!

sections.title: Searching the bookcase by Captain Siad
Talking to Captain Siad

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: What are you doing in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to have a chat?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: You don't belong in here, get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I just need two minutes of your time?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, okay, but very quickly. I am a very busy person you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, er... erm, I err....

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Come on, spit it out! Right that's it! Guards!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, you there!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're in some serious trouble now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my, a dragon just flew straight past your window!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Really! Where? I don't see any dragons young [man/lady]? Now, please get out of my office, I have work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to have a lot of books!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Yes, I do. Now please get to the point?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you been interested in books?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Long enough to know when someone is stalling!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Please state your business or get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could get you some books!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Oh, really!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Sorry, not interested!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're interested in sailing?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, yes actually... It's been a passion of mine for some years...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could tell by the cut of your jib.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Oh yes? Really?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, I was quite a catch in my day you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not much sailing to be done around here though?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well of course there isn't, we're surrounded by desert. Now, why are you here exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it got to do with you?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: This happens to be my office. Now explain yourself before I run you through!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to take your plans, hand them over now or I'll kill you!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The guard downstairs said you were lonely.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, I most certainly am not lonely!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: I'm an incredibly busy man you know! Now, get to the point, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to service your chest.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: You need to what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to service your chest?

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: There's nothing wrong with the chest, it's fine, now get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Prepare to die!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: I'll teach you a lesson!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All the slaves have broken free!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Don't talk rubbish, the warning siren isn't sounding. Now state your business before I have you thrown out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fire! Fire!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Where's the fire? I don't see any fire? Stop messing me around and state your business!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's down in the lower mines, sound the alarm!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: You go and sound the alarm, I can't see anything wrong with the mine. Have you seen the fire yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes actually!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, why didn't you raise the alarm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know where the alarm is.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: That's the most ridiculous thing I've heard. Who are you? Where do you come from? It doesn't matter... Guards! Show this person out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was so concerned for your safety that I rushed to save you.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well, that's very good of you. But as you can see, I am very fine and well thanks! Now, please leave so that I can get back to my work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no, one of the slaves told me.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Well... you can't believe them, they're all a bunch of convicts. Anyway, it doesn't look as if there is a fire down there. So I'm going to get on with my work. Please remove yourself from my office.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, you're right, they must have put it out!

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Good, now perhaps you can leave me in peace? After all I do have some work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yes okay then.

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: Good! Please remove yourself from my office.

sections.title: Searching Captain Siad's desk when he is distracted
Opening chest by Captain Siad

lines.speaker: Captain Siad
lines.text: What are you doing in here?

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim while holding the technical plans

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Aha! I see you have the plans. This is great! However, these plans do indeed look very technical. My people have further need of your skills.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: If you can help us to manufacture this item, we will share its secret with you. Does this deal interest you effendi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm very interested.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, we need the following items: a bar of pure bronze, 10 feathers and a hammer. Bring them to me and we'll continue to make the item.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, we need the following items: a bar of pure bronze and 10 feathers. Bring them to me and we'll continue to make the item.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have a bar of bronze. Now we just need some feathers and a hammer before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have some feathers. Now we just need a bar of bronze and a hammer before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have a bar of bronze. Now we just need some feathers before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have some feathers. Now we just need a bar of bronze before we can continue.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have some feathers and a bar of Bronze, but you just need a hammer now.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Great, I can see that you have some feathers, a bar of Bronze and a hammer. Are you still willing to help make the weapon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm kind of curious.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Okay Effendi, you need to follow the plans. You will need some special tools for this... There is an anvil in the other tent. You have my permission to use it, but show the plans to the guard.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: You have the plans and all the items needed. You should be able to complete the item on your own. Please bring me the item when it is finished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: As you wish Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Come back if you change your mind!

sections.title: Talking to Bedabin Nomad Guard without permission to enter tent

lines.speaker: Bedabin Nomad Guard
lines.text: Sorry, but you can't use the tent without permission. Orders of Al Shabim.

sections.title: Walk-through Tent door without permission

lines.speaker: Bedabin Nomad Guard
lines.text: Sorry, this is a private tent, no one is allowed in. Orders of Al Shabim...

sections.title: Walk-through Tent door after getting permission

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Al Shabim said I could enter, here are the plans!

lines.speaker: Bedabin Nomad Guard
lines.text: Okay go ahead.

sections.title: Walk-through Tent door to leave the tent
Using the experimental anvil
Using feathers on prototype dart tip
Talking to Al Shabim while holding the prototype dart

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: This is truly fantastic Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: We will take the technical plans for the weapon as well.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: We are forever grateful for this gift. My advisors have discovered some secrets which we will share with you.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Please accept this selection of six bronze throwing darts as a token of our appreciation.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: I'll take that key off your hands as well effendi! Many thanks!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Oh, and here is your pineapple!

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim after losing the pineapple

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Many thanks with your help previously Effendi!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for a pineapple.

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Here is another pineapple, try not to lose this one.

sections.title: Talking to Al Shabim with the pineapple in bank or inventory

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Hello Effendi!

lines.speaker: Al Shabim
lines.text: Many thanks with your help previously Effendi!

sections.title: Using a regular pineapple on the guard in the cave

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh great!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yeuch! That's awful! That's not Tenti pineapple! It's got no juice! Get me some Tenti pineapple if you know what's good for you. A whole one so I can see that it's good!

sections.title: Using a pineapple ring on the guard in the cave

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh great!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yeuch! That's awful! That's not Tenti pineapple! It's got no juice! Get me some Tenti pineapple if you know what's good for you. A whole one so I can see that it's good!

sections.title: Using pineapple chunks on the guard in the cave

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh great!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yeuch! That's awful! That's not Tenti pineapple! It's got no juice! Get me some Tenti pineapple if you know what's good for you. A whole one so I can see that it's good!

sections.title: Using the tenti pineapple on the guard in the cave

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey... I have something for you!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Great! Just what I've been looking for! Mmmmmmm... Delicious!! This is soo nice! Mmmmm, *SLURP* Yummmm... Oh yes, this is great.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, can I go through now please?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, yes, of course... a deal's a deal!

sections.title: Talking to the guard in the cave after giving him the tenti pineapple

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: That pineapple was just delicious, many thanks. I don't suppose you could get me another? -- The guard looks at you pleadingly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must be joking! The last one I gave you cost me double shifts working copper ore! You should be grateful you got one at all.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Alright, alright, I was only asking!

sections.title: Using a second tenti pineapple on the guard in the cave

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yeah, whaddya want... get out of here!

sections.title: The deeper mine
Walk through Mine cave
Look in full barrel
Search full barrel
Look in empty barrel
Search empty barrel
Talking to the guard by the winch (dialogue 1)

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Move along now...

sections.title: Talking to the guard by the winch (dialogue 2)

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks - sorry for disturbing you.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well... I guess that's okay, get on your way though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your head on a stick.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Why you ungrateful whelp.. I'll teach you some manners.

sections.title: Look at winch bucket
Using the winch bucket

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey there, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this thing?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It is quite clearly a lift. Any fool can see that it's used to transport rock to the surface.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I use this?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Of course not, you'd be doing me out of a job. Anyway, you haven't got any barrels that need to go to the surface.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Now, move along and get some work done before you get a good beating.

sections.title: Attacking the guard by the winch

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, want a fight do you!

sections.title: Look at mine cart
Searching the mine cart

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: There isn't enough space for both you and this barrel in the cart.

sections.title: Look at mine cart in the eastern room
Searching mine cart in the eastern room

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: There isn't enough space for both you and this barrel in the cart.

sections.title: Search gate to the prison mine
Open gate to enter the prison mine

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, move away from the gate. There's nothing interesting for you here.

sections.title: Open gate to leave the prison mine

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, move away from the gate. If you wanna get out you're gonna have to mine for it. You're gonna have to bring me 15 loads of rocks in one go!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: And then I'll let you out. You can go back to work with the other slaves then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I have all your rocks here, let me out now. -- The guard unlocks the gate. --

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay, okay, come on out.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Okay, you've got all the rocks, you can go now, but keep your nose clean in future.

sections.title: Talking to Ana
Talking to Ana again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hello there, how's it going? Do you have a plan to get out of here yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, sorry, what's your name again?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: My name? Oh, how sweet, my name is Ana. I come from Al Kharid though we've only recently moved there. I was born, and did most of my growing up, in Varrock. I thought the desert might be interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm working on it, have you got any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmmm, let me think...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, have you been in any more trouble with the guards?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: No, not not so far. I've been working very hard. Not like some people I know -- She cocks a smirk at you. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hello there, I don't think I've seen you before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm new here!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I thought so you know! How do you like the hospitality down here? Not exactly Al Kharid town style is it? Well, I guess I'd better get back to work.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Don't want to get into trouble with the guards again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you get into trouble with guards often?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: No, not really, because I'm usually working very hard. Come to think of it, I'd better get back to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you enjoy it down here?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Of course not! I just don't have much choice about it a the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas about how we can get out of here?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmmm, not really, I would have tried them already if I did. The guards seem to live in the compound. How did you get in there anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, see ya!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Goodbye and good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to try and get you out of here!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Wow! You're brave. How do you propose we do that? In case you hadn't noticed, this place is quite well guarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We could try to sneak out.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: That doesn't sound very likely. How did you get in here anyway? Did you deliberately hand yourself over to the guards? Ha, ha ha ha! Sorry, just kidding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to sneak past the guards.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmm, impressive, but can you so easily sneak out again? How did you manage to get through the gate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a key.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I heard that! So you used a key did you?!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Right, we'll have that key off you!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a trade secret!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Oh, right, well, I guess you know what you're doing. Anyway, I have to get back to work. The guards will come along soon and give us some trouble else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh, these guards are rubbish, it was easy to sneak past them!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I heard that! So you managed to sneak in did you!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmmm, let me think...

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: No, sorry...

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: The only thing that gets out of here is the rock that we mine.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Not even the dead get a decent funeral. Bodies are just thrown down disused mine holes. It's very disrespectful...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll check around for another way to try and get out.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does the rock get out?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Well, we mine it in this section, then someone scoops it into a barrel. The barrels are loaded onto a mine cart. Then they're deposited near the surface lift.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I have no idea where they go from there. But that's not going to help us, is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe? I'll come back to you when I have a plan.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Okay, well, I'm not going anywhere! -- Ana nods at a nearby guard!-- Unless he feels generous enough to let me go! -- The guard ignores the comment. --

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Oh well, I'd better get back to work, you take care!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I get one of those barrels from?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Well, you would get one from around by the lift area. But why would you want one of those?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er no reason! Just wondering.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hmmm, just don't get any funny ideas... I am not going to get into one of those barrels! Okay, have you got that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, yep, I've got that.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Good, just make sure you keep it in mind. Anyway, I have to get back to work. The guards will come along soon and give us some trouble else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we'll see, it might be the only way.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: No, there has to be a better way! Anyway, I have to get back to work. The guards will come along soon and give us some trouble else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could try to sneak you out if you were in a barrel!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: There is no way you are getting me into a barrel. No WAY! DO you understand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your name.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: My name? Oh, how sweet, my name is Ana. I come from Al Kharid though we've only recently moved there. I was born, and did most of my growing up, in Varrock. I thought the desert might be interesting.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: What a surprise I got!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of surprise did you get?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Well, I was just touring the desert looking for the nomad tribe to the West. And I was set upon by these armoured men.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I think that the guards think I am an escaped prisoner. They didn't understand that I was exploring the desert as an adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want to go back to Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Sure, I miss my Mum, her name is Irena and she is probably waiting for me. How do you propose we get out of here though?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I'm sure you've noticed the many square jawed guards around here. You look like you can handle yourself, but I have my doubts that you can take them all on!

sections.title: Using the barrel on Ana

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hey, what do you think you're doing?

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: -- You manage to squeeze Ana into the barrel, -- -- despite her many complaints. -- I djont fit in dis bawwel... Wet me out!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shush... It's for your own good!

sections.title: Dropping Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: You drop the barrel on the floor, Ana gets out.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! What's going on here then?

sections.title: Using the barrel on Ana a second time

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hey, what do you think you're doing? Leave me alone and let me get on with my work. Else we'll both be in trouble. Oh no, NOT AGAIN!

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: -- You manage to squeeze Ana into the barrel, -- -- despite her many complaints. -- I djont fit in dis bawwel... Wet me out!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shush... It's for your own good!

sections.title: Look-at Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: -- Ana looks pretty angry, she starts shouting at you. -- Get me out of here! Do you hear me! Get me OUT OF HERE I say!

sections.title: Using Ana in a barrel on the mine cart
Searching empty barrels by the winch after sending Ana
Searching the full barrel by the mining cart after sending Ana

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: Let me out!

sections.title: Using Ana in a barrel on the mine cart again
Using Ana in a barrel on the winch

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, that Barrel looks heavy, do you need a hand?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Blimey! This is heavy!

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: Why you cheeky....!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: ~ The guard looks around, surprised at Ana's outburst. ~ What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, it was nothing.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I could have sworn I heard something!

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: Yes you did you ignoramus.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What was that you said?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I said you were very gregarious!

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: You creep!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, right, how very nice of you to say so. -- The guard seems flattered. --

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Anyway, let's get this barrel up to the surface, plenty more work for you to do!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, there's no one operating the lift up top, hope this barrel isn't urgent? You'd better get back to work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I heard you say something, now spit it out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I can manage.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ok, fair enough, I was only offering.

sections.title: Using the winch after using Ana on it

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, you'd better go and operate that lift yourself if you want that big heavy barrel to go anywhere.

sections.title: Searching empty barrel by the mine cart
Trying to leave out the main entrance while holding Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey, where d'ya think you're going with that Barrel?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Cor! This barrel is really heavy! Have you been mining lead?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Har, har har!

lines.speaker: Ana-in-barrel
lines.text: How rude! Why I ought to teach you a lesson.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What was that!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: How dare you say that I'm as heavy as lead?

sections.title: Returning to the surface
Look at winch
Operate winch with Ana in it

lines.speaker: Ana (in-a-barrel),
lines.text: Get me OUT OF HERE!

sections.title: Operate winch after retrieving Ana
Look-in barrel containing Ana
Search barrel containing Ana

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: Let me out of here, I feel sick!

sections.title: Trying to exit the gates while holding Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! Where d'ya think you're going with that barrel?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You should know that they go out on the cart!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'd better check this out.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Blimey - it's a jail break! Guards! Guards! Apprehend them!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: They're making a break for it!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I could have told you that we wouldn't get away with it! Now look at the mess you've caused.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hey, watch it with the hands buster.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: These are the up market slaves clothes doncha know!

sections.title: Trying to escape through the cell window while holding Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: Don't even think for one minute... you're gonna get me through that window!

sections.title: Look at wooden cart
Search wooden cart while holding Ana in a barrel
Use Ana in a barrel on the wooden cart
Search wooden cart after putting Ana on the cart
Talking to Mine cart driver

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Can't you see I'm busy? Now get out of here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay, sorry.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Look just leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice cart.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Tut !

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder if you could help me?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Sorry friend, I'm busy, go bug the guards, I'm sure they'll give ya the time of day.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: He, he, he, ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you get me the heck out of here please?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: No way, and if you bug me again, I'm gonna call the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One wagon wheel says to the other, 'I'll see you around'.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: -- The cart driver smirks a little. --

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'One good turn deserves another.'

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Are you trying to get me fired?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: It certainly sounds like it, now leave me alone. If you bug me again, I'm gonna call the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: And why would you want to do a crazy thing like that for? I ought to teach you a lesson! Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fired... no, shot perhaps!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Ha ha ha! You're funny! -- The cart driver checks that guards aren't watching. -- What're you in fer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm not supposed to be here at all actually.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Hmmm, interesting... let me guess. You're completely innocent... like all the other inmates in here. Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in for murder, so you'd better get me out of here!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Hmm, well, I wonder what the guards are gonna say about that! Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In for a penny in for a pound.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Ha ha ha, oh stop it!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: What can I do for you anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you smuggle me out on your cart?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Ask that man over there if it's okay and I'll consider it! Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you smuggle my friend out on your cart?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: As long as your friend is a barrel full of rocks. I don't think it would be a problem at all! Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you see, it's like this...

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Prison riot in ten minutes, get your cart out of here!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Oh, right..yes... yess, okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Yeah, you too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't leave me here, I'll get killed!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Oh, right... Okay, you'd better jump in the cart then! Quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's ten gold in it for you if you leave now no questions asked.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: If you're going to bribe me, at least make it worth my while. Now, let's say 100 Gold pieces should we? Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A hundred it is.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: You little cheat, trying to trick me! I'll show you! Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Okay, get in the back of the cart then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Okay, fair enough! But don't bother me anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: I'm quite capable thanks... Now get lost before I call the guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could help, I know a lot about carts.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Are you saying I don't know anything about carts?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Why you cheeky little.... -- The cart driver seems mortally offended -- -- his temper explodes as he calls the guards.-- Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pssst...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Psssst...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Psssssst...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pssssssssttt!!!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: What! Can't you see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay, sorry.

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Look just leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shhshhh!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Shush yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder if you could help me?

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Sorry friend, I'm busy, go bug the guards, I'm sure they'll give ya the time of day.

sections.title: Talking to the Mine cart driver after convincing him of a prison riot
Talking to the Mine cart driver after paying him

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: What're you doing carrying that big barrel around? Put it in the back of the cart like all the others!

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Hurry up, get in the cart or I'll go without you!

sections.title: Searching the wooden cart after paying the Mine cart driver or convincing him of a prison riot

lines.speaker: Mine cart driver
lines.text: Hurry up, get in the cart or I'll go without you!

sections.title: Talking to the Mine cart driver after already escaping once

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should find Ana before attempting to escape again. She must have been caught by the guards.

sections.title: Finishing up
Talking to Irena while holding Ana in a barrel

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Hey, great you've found Ana!

lines.speaker: Ana (in a Barrel)
lines.text: -- You show Irena the barrel with Ana in it. -- Hey great, there's my Mum!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Great! Thanks for getting me out of that mine! And that barrel wasn't too bad anyway! Pop by again sometime, I'm sure we'll have a barrel of laughs!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Oh! I nearly forgot! Here's a key I found in the tunnels. It might be of some use to you, not sure what it opens. Sorry, but I have to go now.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Hi Ana!

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Thank you very much for returning my daughter to me. I'm really very grateful... I would like to reward you for your bravery and daring.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: I can offer you increased knowledge in two of the following areas.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Okay, now choose your second skill.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Okay, that's all the skills I can teach you!

sections.title: Talking to Irena before getting the wrought iron key

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Thanks for returning Anna to me, I think she has something else to say to you though!

sections.title: Talking to Ana before getting the wrought iron key

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Great! Thanks for getting me out of that mine! And that barrel wasn't too bad anyway! Pop by again sometime, I'm sure we'll have a barrel of laughs!

sections.title: Talking to Irena after rescuing Ana

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Thank you very much for returning my daughter to me. I'm really very grateful... I would like to reward you for your bravery and daring. I can offer you increased knowledge in two of the following areas.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Okay, now choose your second skill.

sections.title: Talking to Irena after choosing the first skill

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Thank you very much for returning my daughter to me. I'm really very grateful... I would like to reward you for your bravery and daring. I can offer you increased knowledge in one of the following areas.

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Okay, that's all the skills I can teach you!

sections.title: Talking to Irena after choosing both skills

lines.speaker: Irena
lines.text: Thanks so much for returning my daughter to me. I expect that she will go on another trip soon though. She is the adventurous type... a bit like yourself really! Okay, see you around then!
